chunks.text: Undergraduate Scholarships - EECS at Berkeley Undergraduate Scholarships A selected listing of scholarships for undergraduate engineering students. In This Page Scholarships for everyone Scholarships for veterans and those interested in the military

selected conferences in Computer Science and related fields. Grant eligibility varies by region, so please review the information on the region-specific pages. IEEE Students Funding Search You can search the Institute of Electrical and Electronics E

arch the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE)’s database for awards, fellowships, grants, and scholarships. NASA Internships National Aeronatics and Space Administration (NASA) internships and fellowships seek to enhance and incre

ernships and fellowships seek to enhance and increase the capability, diversity and size of the nation’s future science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) workforce. Internships are available from high school to graduate level which prov

able from high school to graduate level which provide students with the opportunity to participate in either research or other experiential learning, under the guidance of a mentor at NASA. UC Berkeley’s Scholarship Connection UC Berkeley’s clearingh

y’s Scholarship Connection UC Berkeley’s clearinghouse for information on scholarships that are funded by sources outside the University. Their website shows application deadlines and allows students to search the Scholarship Connection online datab

to search the Scholarship Connection online database. Veteran and Military Scholarships Google Student Veterans of America Scholarships For EECS graduate and undergraduate students that have received an honorable discharge, or are in good standing w

an honorable discharge, or are in good standing with their branch of service. AFCEA Scholarships to Advance Global Security The Armed Forces Communications and Electronics Association (AFCEA) award grants to students enrolled full-time in degree-gr

grants to students enrolled full-time in degree-granting programs in fields directly related to the support of U.S. intelligence or homeland security enterprises, and/or foreign languages. Tips for Successful Applications The Application Tips on the

ccessful Applications The Application Tips on the Summer Research page work for scholarship applications as well. Resources Information for Current Students Broadening Participation in EECS Department Policy on Academic Misconduct Wellness Resources
